# Plan — 2026-05-30

Source research: `.github/research/2026-05-30-research.md`.

All seven carry-overs from 2026-05-29 are closed in `src/`. This cycle ships
five small/medium parity items inspired by claude-code v2.1.157–158 (May 30),
kilocode CLI 7.3.16–7.3.18 (May 29), and cline #11139 (May 29). The
sixth research item (per-directory AGENTS.md as `<system-reminder>`) is the
highest-leverage behaviour change but the research itself recommends
specifying it as its own plan in the next cycle — deferred.

## Issues created

| # | Issue | Title | Category | Size | Source |
|---|-------|-------|----------|------|--------|
| 1 | [#530](https://github.com/ausardcompany/alexi/issues/530) | [tool] Auto-extract DOCX and XLSX text in the `read` tool | tool | small (< 4h) | kilocode#10737/#10740 |
| 2 | [#533](https://github.com/ausardcompany/alexi/issues/533) | [tool] OSC-8 clickable URL helper for the TUI | tool | small (< 2h) | cline#11139 |
| 3 | [#531](https://github.com/ausardcompany/alexi/issues/531) | [config] Honor `agent` field in user config as default agent | config | small (< 4h) | claude-code v2.1.157 |
| 4 | [#534](https://github.com/ausardcompany/alexi/issues/534) | [hook] `SessionStart` `reloadSkills` output and `/reload-skills` command | hook + command | small (< 4h) | claude-code v2.1.152 |
| 5 | [#532](https://github.com/ausardcompany/alexi/issues/532) | [plugin] Auto-load `.alexi/skills/<name>/plugin.json` and add `alexi plugin init` scaffolder | plugin | medium (1–2 days) | claude-code v2.1.157 |

## Notes

- Issues 1–4 touch independent files and have no inter-dependencies; they
  can land as four parallel PRs in a single day.
- Issue 5 is medium effort; recommended split as two sub-PRs (auto-load
  first, scaffolder second). Issue 4 (`/reload-skills`) makes the auto-load
  side of issue 5 usable mid-session — sequence 4 → 5(a) → 5(b).
- Per-directory AGENTS.md as `<system-reminder>` (research item #1) is
  deferred to the next cycle as advised by the research doc itself.
- POSIX `cd`/`pushd`/subshell permission audit (carry-over from 2026-05-29)
  was not re-filed this cycle; recommend filing as a separate tracking
  issue outside the auto-implement queue.

## Constraints respected

- All five issues are fully self-contained with file paths, exact APIs,
  and acceptance criteria.
- No similar `auto-implement` issues open at planning time
  (`gh issue list --label auto-implement --state open` returned nothing).
- Categories used: `tool`, `config`, `hook`, `command`, `plugin` — all
  inside the allowed set.
